description: Fresh fruits and vegetables for cooking, salads, and healthy snacks.
Meat, poultry, fish, eggs, and plant-based proteins to help fill out meals.
donor_friendly_translation: Meat, fish, eggs, and plant protein

description: Milk, cheese, yogurt, and other refrigerated dairy for everyday meals.
Rice, pasta, bread, cereal, and other pantry staples that stretch groceries.
Pantry basics without wheat or gluten — for celiac disease or gluten sensitivity.
Canned beans, soups, tomatoes, and other shelf-stable items that keep well.
Formula, baby food, and infant supplies for families with young children.
Diapers and related infant supplies — a frequent high-need item for families.
Soap, toothpaste, toilet paper, and laundry supplies — not food, but essential for households.
Crackers, granola bars, juice, and other items for kids and quick bites between meals.
